destinations.evidence: The shared async-fees constants define the deliveryResult ABI offset as parameter index 10 for Phase 2 delivery settlement calldata.
The verifier classifies delivery calldata and extracts deliveryResult using the shared 10 * 32 offset, so Phase 1/Phase 2 classification uses the corrected parameter.

equivalence_argument: The port moved the offset into a shared constant and the verifier consumes that constant for AsyncSettlementCall::Deliver, preserving the parameter-10 delivery-result length rule.
rationale: The commit message identifies the offset bug as `9*32 -> 10*32`. The verifier and transaction pool now document the 11-parameter layout and read the deliveryResult offset at `10 * 32`, which controls Phase 1 vs Phase 2 settlement classification. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: For SC-2 settlement calldata with 11 parameters, delivery-result length extraction must read the dynamic bytes offset at parameter index 10, not parameter index 9, so empty delivery results are treated as Phase 1 and non-empty delivery results as Phase 2 removals.
suggested_falsification_tests: Encode `settleAsyncTransaction(..., precompileInput = nonempty, deliveryResult = empty)` and assert delivery-result length extraction returns 0; encode a Phase 2 call with nonempty deliveryResult and assert it returns that result length.
summary: Delivery result extraction uses the shared parameter-10 offset in the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Encode delivery calldata with precompileInput nonempty and deliveryResult empty/nonempty and assert extract_delivery_result_from_calldata reports the parameter-10 bytes length.
title: Delivery result length uses parameter ten
